designer: Chris McGinley (SOURCE OF TRUTH for all feature names and specs)
naming_source: Chris McGinley product deck — names are pre-defined, not AI-generated
micro_script_framework: Bill Schley 'The Micro-Script Rules' — DSI-first, ~5.2 word avg, repeat-test validated

dsi.statement: The only premium putter with Forward Axis Weighting that eliminates face drift and improves your stroke at the same time.

validation.important: Eliminates face drift (why you miss) + improves your stroke (dual promise)
validation.believable: Forward Axis Weighting is named, real, designed by 25-year OEM vet Chris McGinley
validation.measurable: Face Down Balance test proves it, stroke improvement is feelable
validation.ownable: No one else has Forward Axis Weighting

putter_level_micro_scripts.primary_tagline: Pull it back, let it fall.

umbrella_system.name: Roll Straight Technology
umbrella_system.description: Roll Straight Technology is the complete engineering system that keeps the face square to the target line — combining Forward Axis Weighting, Face Down Balance, upright lie geometry, and multi-material construction into a single integrated design. Nine features, one objective: roll straight.
umbrella_system.position: Mentioned first, sits above all individual features. The umbrella that unifies the feature story.

hero.name: Forward Axis Weighting
hero.capability: 75%+ of the 360g head weight positioned in the front 25% of the putter head, placing the center of gravity ahead of the shaft axis
hero.what_it_does: Reverses the gravitational torque present in every other putter — instead of gravity pulling the face open, gravity pulls the face toward square
hero.why_it_matters: Eliminates equipment-induced face drift AND actively assists the natural putting stroke (dual promise: technology + technique)
micro_scripts: Pull it back, let it fall.

competitive_spectrum.rear_axis: Face drifts open — player fights it — gravity works against you
competitive_spectrum.center_axis_lab: Face hangs neutral — player controls it — passive (zero torque)
competitive_spectrum.forward_axis_rs1: Face stays square — gravity controls it — active assist (zero face drift)

dual_promise.point_1_technology: Zero face drift — gravity holds the face square through the stroke
dual_promise.point_2_technique: Active gravity assist — pull back, let it fall, gravity does the work

hero_proof.name: Face Down Balance
hero_proof.capability: When placed on a flat surface, the RS1 face sits DOWN instead of up — proving the center of gravity is ahead of the shaft
hero_proof.what_it_does: Provides a visible, repeatable, 5-second physical test that proves Forward Axis Weighting is real — not a marketing claim
hero_proof.why_it_matters: The putter proves itself before you hit a single putt. No faith required — only observation.
hero_proof.relationship_to_hero: FDB is the visual PROOF of Forward Axis Weighting — not a separate mechanism. Forward Axis Weighting is the engineering. FDB is what you can see and verify.
hero_proof.demo_instruction: Place RS1 face-down on any flat surface. Face stays down. Repeat with any other putter. Face sits up. That is the difference.
hero_proof.source: pg-rs1-product-deck.md — Hero Feature: Face Down Balance section

supporting.name: Square-to-Square Lie Angle
supporting.name_change_note: Renamed from '74-Degree Upright Lie Angle' to 'Square-to-Square Lie Angle' — 74° is the technical description, not the feature name
supporting.capability: Lie angle 4 degrees more upright than industry standard (~70 degrees), reducing stroke arc
supporting.what_it_does: Creates a more straight-back, straight-through putting motion with less face rotation during the stroke
supporting.why_it_matters: Double reduction in face drift — Forward Axis Weighting resists face opening AND the upright lie reduces how much the face opens in the first place
supporting.supporting_detail: Combined with Forward Axis Weighting, creates the technique assist pillar of the dual promise. Interchangeable shaft spuds are precision-milled to exact lie angle.
supporting.source: pg-rs1-product-deck.md — Supporting Features: Square to Square Lie Angle

supporting.name: 10-Piece Premium Construction
supporting.capability: Multi-material construction: CNC-machined steel face, carbon composite woven crown, CNC-machined aluminum tail, plus shaft spuds, weight cups, alignment inserts, and grip
supporting.what_it_does: Enables extreme Forward Axis Weighting that is impossible with single-material construction — heavy where weight matters, light where it doesn't
supporting.why_it_matters: The engineering precision that makes Forward Axis Weighting possible. Every gram is where Chris McGinley placed it.
component_breakdown: CNC-machined steel face (heavy — drives Forward Axis Weighting)
Carbon composite woven crown (light — saves weight from top)
CNC-machined aluminum tail (light — saves weight from rear)
Precision-milled shaft spuds (interchangeable, exact lie angle)
supporting.source: pg-rs1-product-deck.md — Harmonized Multi Material (renamed per human direction)

supporting.name: Patented Dual Pistol Grip
supporting.capability: The only dual-sided pistol grip in the market — ergonomically CAD-engineered with flat top/bottom and large curved sides
supporting.what_it_does: Locks squarely into the hands' lifeline, reduces grip pressure, and minimizes overactive hand movement through the stroke
supporting.why_it_matters: Quieter hands mean less interference with Forward Axis Weighting's gravity-driven face control
supporting.superlative_claim: The ONLY dual-sided pistol grip in golf
supporting.supporting_detail: Works equally well for conventional, cross-hand, and claw grips. Available in rubber (Standard) or PU (Upgraded). Encourages ulnar deviation for a more stable stroke.
supporting.source: pg-rs1-product-deck.md — Patented Dual Pistol Grip section

supporting.name: Face Forward Design
supporting.capability: Shaft positioned behind the face, providing full unobstructed face visibility at address
supporting.what_it_does: Creates full face visibility for alignment and a natural pendulum pivot point for the stroke
supporting.why_it_matters: See the entire face at setup — no hosel or shaft blocking your view. The pivot point enables a pure pendulum motion.

supporting.name: Face Square Focus
supporting.capability: Three extended parallel alignment lines running tail-to-front, converging at a perpendicular face line
supporting.what_it_does: Makes misalignment visible before the stroke starts — three lines reveal face angle error more effectively than a single line
supporting.why_it_matters: You see the problem before you create it. Setup confidence becomes automatic.
supporting.benefit_tagline: Line it up, lock it in.
micro_scripts: Line it up, lock it in.

technical.name: T-Wing Tail
technical.capability: Sculpted T-shaped tail section with lightweight aluminum construction
technical.what_it_does: Saves weight from the rear of the putter head, redistributing it forward to enable extreme Forward Axis Weighting
technical.why_it_matters: The distinctive RS1 silhouette — where form follows function. Every gram removed from the tail is a gram added to the face.
technical.benefit_tagline: Less tail weight, more face weight.
micro_scripts: Less tail weight, more face weight.
The shape isn't just design, it's engineering.
technical.source: pg-rs1-product-deck.md — Bold & Beautiful T-Wing (renamed per human direction)

technical.name: Sculpted Sole Relief
technical.capability: Three zones of sole relief — front, middle, and back — with rounded leading/trailing edges and a non-contact midsole
technical.what_it_does: Allows the putter to glide back and through without catching or dragging on the putting surface
technical.why_it_matters: Clean contact with the green surface throughout the stroke. No snag. No drag. Just glide.

naming_validation.all_names_source: Chris McGinley product deck (SOURCE OF TRUTH) with human-directed updates: Heavy Forward Weighting → Forward Axis Weighting, Harmonized Multi Material → 10-Piece Premium Construction, Bold & Beautiful T-Wing → T-Wing Tail, 74-Degree Upright Lie Angle → Square-to-Square Lie Angle

page_flow: Roll Straight Technology (umbrella intro — what the system is)
Forward Axis Weighting (hero mechanism — what it does)
Face Down Balance (hero proof — see it for yourself)
Patented Dual Pistol Grip (supporting — grip stability)
feature_story_architecture.narrative_logic: Umbrella (what) → Hero (why) → Proof (see) → Supporting (how) → Technical (details). Each feature builds on the previous. Forward Axis Weighting creates the need for the construction explanation. FDB proves it works. Lie angle extends the benefit. Construction shows the engineering. Design features complete the picture.

ec_02_hero.dsi: The only premium putter with Forward Axis Weighting that eliminates face drift and improves your stroke at the same time.
hero_micro_scripts: Pull it back, let it fall.
ec_02_hero.campaign_tagline: Pull it back, let it fall.

ec_04_scripts.note: 13 total micro-scripts across 9 features (F-001 and F-008 have 2 each, plus 4 putter-level scripts)

gate_status.gate_2_5: HUMAN_PRE_APPROVED — feature names locked from Chris McGinley product deck with human-directed updates. Arena adapted: names are SOURCE OF TRUTH, arena evaluated micro-scripts and taglines only.

session_state.human_selection_note: Feature names pre-approved by human operator across multiple sessions. Names come from Chris McGinley product deck with specific human corrections applied. Micro-scripts created using Bill Schley DSI-first framework and approved one-by-one by human operator.
session_state.next_action: EC-02 (Hero & Value Prop) — use DSI + hero feature + proof + umbrella for ATF copy
